closure.title: Closure of activity
closure.content:
The Dignitat a les Vies platform regrets to announce that we are forced to cease operations on our data portal regarding the status of the conventional railway network. Adif has confirmed that it has requested the blocking of our website, arguing that information regarding Temporary Speed Restrictions (TSR) is "sensitive" and should not be known by the general public.

We consider this decision an act of unacceptable paternalism that distances the Spanish State from European standards. While other European Union countries provide public portals where this data is published openly, authorities here have chosen opacity. The fact that this portal was created in less than a single working day proves that the Government's failure to provide such information is due to a clear lack of interest, not technical limitations. In contrast, the response from professionals was immediate: within the first 24 hours, several railway workers submitted ten separate data contributions, demonstrating that the sector's own staff has a genuine interest in making the state of the tracks public.

Furthermore, we strongly believe that Adif is failing to comply with the current legal framework. According to Spain's Law 9/2025 on Sustainable Mobility, EU Regulation 2017/1926 (MMTIS) —as amended by 2024/490— and Directive (UE) 2019/1024, data concerning infrastructure and its restrictions must be made available as open data. Understanding the state of the network not only helps users comprehend chronic delays, but it is also the only way to verify, with hard data, that the tracks we travel on every day are safe. Railway safety cannot be a state secret; transparency is the greatest guarantee of public trust.

Various media outlets have published similar maps and statistics using the same sources, and we remind the authorities that the freedom of information protects us all equally. We will not stop advocating for our right to know and to travel with dignity.
